channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket has a problem and it's leaving the launch pad. Don't expect a moonshot in March - Space
item.description:
1. NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket has a problem and it's leaving the launch pad. Don't expect a moonshot in March  Space
  2. NASA to Rollback Artemis II Rocket, Spacecraft  NASA (.gov)
  3. NASA Postpones Artemis II Launch After Finding Helium Flow Issue, Just Days After Successful Wet Dress Rehearsal  IFLScience
  4. What four astronauts could find as they journey around the moon and beyond its far side  CNN
  5. NASA Delays Launch of Artemis II Lunar Mission Once Again  WIRED

item.title: Scientists Simulated The Big Bang's Aftermath, And Found The Universe Was Like Soup - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists Simulated The Big Bang's Aftermath, And Found The Universe Was Like Soup  ScienceAlert
  2. Physicists recreated the first millisecond after the Big Bang — and found it was surprisingly soupy  Live Science
  3. Scientists Just Recreated the Big Bang's First Moments, and It’s More Complex Than We Thought  The Daily Galaxy
  4. Scientists recreate conditions of first millisecond after "Big Bang" inside particle collider  Caliber.Az
  5. Scientists recreate the first millisecond after the Big Bang at the LHC and find a drop of less than 1%, revealing what the primordial "soup" of the universe was like.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
